race_name: Bob Cook Memorial Mt. Evans Hill Climb (Blue Sky Hill Climb)

top_categories.workout_context: 6,590ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Bob Cook Memorial Mt. Evans Hill Climb (Blue Sky Hill Climb)'s demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.

Bob Cook Memorial Mt. Evans Hill Climb (Blue Sky Hill Climb)'s paved mountain highway to extreme altitude forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 27 miles.
27 miles on paved mountain highway to extreme altitude burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Base fitness for Bob Cook Memorial Mt. Evans Hill Climb (Blue Sky Hill Climb). Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved mountain highway to extreme altitude. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,590ft of climbing on paved mountain highway to extreme altitude does to your power file on race day.
241ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Bob Cook Memorial Mt. Evans Hill Climb (Blue Sky Hill Climb), that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Below LT1, you burn fat. Above it, you burn glycogen. For 27 miles, a higher LT1 means slower bonk.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Bob Cook Memorial Mt. Evans Hill Climb (Blue Sky Hill Climb). Train it until it feels boring.
At Bob Cook Memorial Mt. Evans Hill Climb (Blue Sky Hill Climb), surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.

race_overlay.nutrition: Standard hydration and fueling. One bottle per hour minimum. Gel or bar every 45 minutes at race intensity.
race_overlay.altitude: Bob Cook Memorial Mt. Evans Hill Climb (Blue Sky Hill Climb) with 6,590ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. Temperature drop of 30-40F from start to summit — hypothermia is a real risk; 40%+ reduction in oxygen at the summit — breathing is labored even standing still.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 27 miles of paved mountain highway to extreme altitude in Idaho Springs, Colorado.
